episodes.title: Former Model Exposes Cult Life
episodes.description:
Former ’80s supermodel Hoyt Richards reveals the dark truth behind his secret life in the cult-like group Eternal Values, led by charismatic but manipulative Frederick von Mierers—whose real identity was Fred Meyers from Brooklyn. Once a Princeton football star turned global model, Richards poured his earnings into the group, believing he was part of an elite chosen to survive the apocalypse. But as the docuseries uncovers, followers endured extreme control: strict diets, severed family ties, and even coerced sexual encounters—all while being sold expensive “gem prescriptions” that sparked a legal probe. After von Mierers’ death, Richards’ doubts were met with emotional abuse, forcing him to flee. With the help of fellow model Fabio, he found healing and now helps others escape cults, reclaiming his story from the shadows of manipulation.

episodes.title: Nicholas Galitzine Joins Gus Van Sant's Cult Drama
episodes.description:
Nicholas Galitzine, fresh off his Masters of the Universe fame, is diving headfirst into a gritty new role as Hoyt Richards — a once-glamorous supermodel ensnared by a doomsday cult — in Gus Van Sant’s upcoming film, based on the chilling real-life story featured in HBO’s Bring Me the Beauties: A Model Cult. With Galitzine buzzing about the project and Van Sant at the helm, this could be a dark, intense exploration of isolation, manipulation, and redemption in the fashion world — all while he juggles his blockbuster roles.

episodes.title: A Cult on Public Access TV
episodes.description:
Step into the gritty, grainy world of New York’s forgotten public access TV era with “Bring Me the Beauties,” a haunting HBO documentary that uncovers the rise, fall, and lasting echo of a cult-like self-help group. Using deliberately low-res archival footage to evoke authentic 80s/90s vibes, the film mirrors the Wham! documentary’s visual strategy—degrading quality to match the era’s raw aesthetic. Spanning three episodes, it traces the group’s hopeful beginnings, their chilling collapse, and the lingering aftermath, revealing how one person’s spiritual quest became a magnet for devoted followers—and how easily we all fall into influence, whether we realize it or not. Premiering June 1 on HBO, and June 2 on Max in the UK and Australia, this is more than a story—it’s a mirror.

episodes.title: Holly Madison on Playboy Mansion: Cult-Like & Traumatic
episodes.description:
Holly Madison, former Girls Next Door star, shares her experiences living in the Playboy Mansion on Kristin Cavallaris podcast. She discusses the shift in dynamics once the reality show began, leading to changes in routine and Hugh Hefners renewed sense of relevance. Madison also touches on the darker aspects of her time there, comparing it to a cult-like environment with traumatic moments. She left in 2008 and Hefner passed away in 2017. Playboy Enterprises supports women sharing their experiences, acknowledging the tough stories of sexual violence.

episodes.title: Andrew Keegan Defends Full Circle: Not a Cult, a Spiritual Community
episodes.description:
Actor Andrew Keegan defends Full Circle group, dismissing cult rumors. He emphasizes it was a spiritual community focused on healing and connection, inspired by Occupy Wall Street protests. Keegan blames media for misrepresenting the group, which he says was a safe space for all, prioritizing true values over the daily grind.

episodes.title: Gothard's Silence: Duggar Family's Cult-Like Teachings
episodes.description:
Joseph Duggar and his family face criticism for their strict religious practices, likened to a cult, while Bill Gothard, the founder of the Institute in Basic Life Principles, remains silent. Gothard, accused of sexual harassment and molestation by thirty-four women, stepped down but admitted to inappropriate behavior. Now, ex-followers like Lara Smith share stories of assaults, blaming teachings that blame women and protect men.
